exceptions.drafted_by: agent session, from an owner instruction given in chat on 2026-07-20
exceptions.provenance_caveat: An agent wrote this record. The owner selected 'document as accepted solo-owner risk' in a chat session, which is an instruction to draft, not the owner-review process this repository defines. A chat selection is not a named owner approval, carries no independent reviewer, and synchronizes no registry. Until the owner countersigns through the documented process, `accepted_by` stays null and this record is a draft. Do not cite it as an owner decision.
countersignature_requirements: owner confirms the acceptance in a durable repository record rather than a chat session
record names the owner and the date of countersignature
record is reconciled with the owner-decision closure board
exceptions.claim_status: Agent-drafted single-owner residual-risk record pending owner countersignature; no owner acceptance, no backup ownership, no owner coverage, no two-person control, no release authority claim, no release promotion claim, no stable signing authority claim, no update trust claim, no supported-version changes claim, no security-disclosure authority claim, no irreversible migration approval claim, no legal approval claim, no incident closure authority claim, no production authority claim, no broad readiness claim, and no implementation claim.
exceptions.why_gate_remains_blocked: validate_blueprint.py enforces PB-019 blocked while backup ownership is only checked gap evidence and while any professional owner backup is null. The PB-019 evidence_required list names single-owner residual risk as a reason to remain blocked. Owner acceptance of a risk is not remediation of that risk.
accepted_exposure: no second reviewer for any build-critical scope
no independent review of the primary owner's own changes
simultaneous halt of every scope on owner unavailability
no recusal path for conflict, legal conflict, or security embargo
no reviewed second holder of access, signing material, or disclosure channels
unrecovered single point of failure on account or credential loss
compensating_controls: work stays inside the contained-M0 boundary
no release, signing, publishing, or distribution path is exercised
no production or user-facing data is handled
tools/validate_*.py checks act as a mechanical reviewer for structural consistency only, never for judgement or authority
owner-only decisions remain queued on the owner-decision closure board
revisit_triggers: any move toward developer preview, beta, stable, or public distribution
any request to exercise signing, update-trust, or supported-version changes
receipt of a security report requiring coordinated disclosure
any irreversible profile or data migration proposal
acceptance of ADR-0009 or any decision opening broad M1 implementation
addition of any second contributor with write access
exceptions.expiry_behavior: On expiry the owner must name qualified backups, renew with a fresh dated record, or narrow the scope of work. An expired acceptance is not self-renewing; treat solo operation as unaccepted and stop contained-M0 continuation until refreshed.
prohibited_uses: citing this record as backup ownership or owner coverage
citing this record as two-person control
citing this record as release authority, release promotion, or stable signing authority
citing this record as update-trust or supported-version authority
citing this record as security-disclosure or incident-closure authority
citing this record as irreversible migration approval or legal approval
citing this record as production authority, broad readiness, or implementation approval
citing this record to promote PB-019 out of blocked status
exceptions.next_proof: A named qualified backup owner for at least one build-critical scope carrying role level, subsystem competence, representative path coverage, recent review record, availability, succession, recusal, inactivity, removal, and emergency-replacement evidence, reconciled against CODEOWNERS and the professional review rules.
